macro.takeaway: Inflation picked up in April, which matters because it can keep interest rates higher for longer (usually a headwind for stocks and bonds).

gold.takeaway: Gold slipped over the past week, but it often stays in focus as a ‘shock absorber’ when investors worry about inflation or geopolitical risks.

china.takeaway: China A-shares may be holiday-closed on some days, so this uses the US-listed ASHR ETF as a proxy; China’s April retail sales grew only +0.2% y/y, suggesting consumer demand is still soft.

source.name: National Bureau of Statistics of China (Retail Sales)

thailand.takeaway: Thai stocks were a bit higher, while the Bank of Thailand kept its policy rate at 1.00% to support a slowing economy.

us.takeaway: U.S. stocks ended the week near highs as investors weighed inflation data against a Fed that is still holding rates at 3.5%–3.75%.
